sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_table (id INTEGER, name VARCHAR, created_at DATE, value NUMERIC(10,2));
INSERT INTO test_table VALUES (1, 'test1', DATE '2021-01-01', 10.00), (2, 'sample', DATE '2022-06-15', 20.50), (3, 'demo', DATE '2023-08-05', NULL);
SELECT id, regexp_replace(name, '[aeiou]', '#') AS replaced_name, created_at + 30 AS new_date, value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, regexp_replace(name, '[^a-z]', '') AS filtered_name, created_at + CAST(value AS INTEGER) AS incremented_date FROM test_table HAVING LENGTH(filtered_name) > 0;
SELECT id, regexp_replace(name, '[s]', 'S') AS updated_name FROM (SELECT id, name FROM test_table WHERE created_at < DATE '2022-01-01') subquery;
CREATE TABLE another_table (ref_id INTEGER REFERENCES test_table(id), ref_value VARCHAR);
SELECT t.id, r.ref_value, regexp_replace(t.name, '[^ ]', 'X') AS obscured_name FROM test_table t JOIN another_table r ON t.id = r.ref_id WHERE regexp_replace(t.name, 'e', '') IS NULL;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) AS value_count FROM test_table GROUP BY id HAVING COUNT(*) + 1 = 2;
INSERT INTO test_table (id, name, created_at, value) VALUES (4, '', DATE '2020-01-01', 99.99);
SELECT created_at, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY created_at) AS count_per_date FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, regexp_replace(name, 'a', '') || (created_at + INTERVAL '10 days') AS complex_expression FROM test_table;
SELECT id, value + NULL AS null_test FROM test_table;
SELECT id FROM test_table WHERE regexp_replace(name, '[']', '') = '' AND value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, regexp_replace(name, '[abc]', '#') AS modification, created_at + value FROM test_table WHERE value IS NOT NULL;
